buildings.name: Maize Chinampa
buildings.description: Raised canal-fed maize plots. The estate food base and the first real production engine.

buildings.name: Cacao Garden
buildings.description: Protected garden plots for cacao. Valuable for ritual, nobles, tribute and market pressure.

buildings.name: Cotton Chinampa
buildings.description: Cotton plots that feed the cloth and fine textile chains.

buildings.name: Woodcutting Yard
buildings.description: A managed woodland work yard that brings timber into the estate economy. It belongs under Production > Workshops because it is a work site rather than a chinampa, but it is staffed by ordinary Field Labour rather than Tolteca artisans.

buildings.name: Obsidian Quarry Access
buildings.description: Quarry access and rough obsidian extraction for the estate. It appears under Production > Workshops because it is a specialised work site, but it uses ordinary Field Labour rather than craftsmen.

buildings.name: Basic Tool Workshop
buildings.description: A small starter workshop that prevents the estate from becoming tool-paralysed.

buildings.name: Cloth Workshop
buildings.description: Converts cotton and tools into cloth for upkeep, buildings and palace-facing production.

buildings.name: Weapon Yard
buildings.description: Produces weapons from obsidian, wood, cloth and tools. This is expensive but opens war readiness.

buildings.name: Fine Textile House
buildings.description: Produces elite palace-facing fine textiles from cotton, cacao and tools.

buildings.name: Commoner Housing
buildings.description: Basic calpulli-style housing and support for commoners and estate workers. This is the first housing pressure release for estate growth.

buildings.name: Tlacotin Quarters
buildings.description: Quarters and support space required before enslaved or bonded labour can be used at larger scale.

buildings.name: Warrior House
buildings.description: Housing and support for warriors. This belongs to the Housing screen; active military preparation belongs to the Barracks screen.

buildings.name: Priest House
buildings.description: Housing and support for priests and ritual servants. Priests need material support before shrine operation can scale.

buildings.name: Noble Residence
buildings.description: A status residence for nobles, palace-facing activity and elite obligations.

buildings.name: Captive Holding Pen
buildings.description: A guarded holding place for captives taken through Flower Wars. Captive holding is a capacity pressure, not an ordinary labour building.
